name: US Army Drafts Apple Xserve for Supercomputer
description: MacNewsWorld coverage of the U.S. Army's MACH 5 supercomputer built on Apple Xserve G5 (via COLSA), projected at ~25 teraflops and ranked second after DOE systems at the time. Peter Kastner, executive vice president and chief research officer at Aberdeen Group, calls the deal a 'wakeup call' and declares Apple 'not a one-shot wonder, but a force to be reckoned with' in supercomputing after Virginia Tech's earlier System X deployment.

creator.name: Blane Warrene (reporter, MacNewsWorld); Peter Kastner (quoted source)
